stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results being announced TODAY (April 29, 2026) - board meeting in progress. Pre-announcement consensus: Revenue Rs 44,000-46,000 cr (vs Rs 43,921 cr in Q3 FY26), PAT Rs 3,000-3,600 cr. Stock at Rs 1,892.50 with market cap Rs 11.23 lakh cr; intraday +Rs 28.5 (+1.5%) vs previous close Rs 1,843.80. Recent strategic wins: Google broke ground on $15bn Vizag AI hub with Nxtra by Airtel and AdaniConneX. Airtel Money received NBFC license from RBI on February 13, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Indian telecom set up for major tariff cycle: Morgan Stanley expects 16-20% prepaid/postpaid tariff hikes in 2026 across Jio, Airtel, Vi. Airtel ARPU at Rs 256 in Q2 FY26; could cross Rs 300 with next hike, Rs 400 in subsequent cycle. Industry quietly tweaking plans ahead of major hike. Premiumisation and enterprise 5G are key 2026 drivers. Vi's 'you-first-then-us' stance favors duopoly economics for Jio/Airtel.
stocks.peer_comparison: Airtel and Jio gaining share at Vi's expense. Airtel's data center optionality (Nxtra/Google Vizag deal) and Airtel Money NBFC license differentiate it from Jio's pure telco-plus-platform play and Vi's struggling balance sheet. Stock 13% off 52-week high of Rs 2,174.50 - room to re-rate vs analyst target band Rs 1,800-2,100.
red_flags: Stock has retraced 13% from 52-week high of Rs 2,174.50 - momentum cooled
Q4 results being announced today is a binary event - any miss vs Rs 3,000-3,600 cr PAT range could trigger sell-off
Broader Nifty under 24,000 with FII outflows and 26% US reciprocal tariff overhang
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results announcement TODAY (April 29, 2026) - PAT, ARPU, dividend in focus
16-20% tariff hike expected in 2026 - Morgan Stanley estimate
Google Vizag AI hub partnership build-out with Nxtra
Airtel Money NBFC license enables financial services scale-up

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results approved April 24, 2026: PAT Rs 940 cr (+106% YoY vs Rs 456 cr); Total Income Rs 5,560 cr (+14% YoY). FY26 PAT Rs 2,861 cr (+27% YoY); FY26 Total Income Rs 21,087 cr (+14%). Asset quality improved: Stage 3 assets 3.4%, Stage 2 assets 4.8% (gradual normalization, slippage control). Final dividend Rs 7.50/share (375% on Rs 2 face value, vs Rs 6.50 last year), subject to AGM on July 21, 2026. Stock closed at Rs 312.65 on April 28, 2026 with market cap Rs 44,159 cr - has cooled off from April 27 intraday high of Rs 327.6 (post-results 11.3% rally).
stocks.sector_context: Auto-financing NBFCs benefiting from rural recovery, healthy disbursement growth, and parent M&M's strong tractor/SUV portfolio. NBFC sector bifurcating - quality names (M&MFIN, Bajaj Finance, Chola) outperforming while gold/microfinance names (IIFL) face regulatory pressure. RBI's revised gold loan norms add compliance costs to that subsegment.
stocks.peer_comparison: Sharp outperformance vs NBFC peers post-results - 11% rally on April 27 vs IIFL's -9% drop the same day on tax issues. Earnings growth (+106% YoY PAT) strongest in NBFC peer set. Trading at reasonable valuation vs Bajaj Finance (premium) and Chola.
red_flags: Stock ran up 11.3% on April 27, then cooled ~5% to Rs 312.65 by April 28 close - profit-booking risk persists
Higher base effect could moderate growth optics in coming quarters
Rural/auto demand cyclicality - asset quality could pressure if monsoon disappoints
FY26 PAT growth of 27% lags Q4 +106% - run-rate moderation likely
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 7.50/share with AGM record date around July 21, 2026
Rural recovery and FY27 disbursement guidance
M&M parent's auto sales momentum supports captive financing volumes
Continued asset quality normalization (Stage 3 trending down)

stocks.recent_news: Nippon India ETF Nifty 50 BeES at Rs 271.09 as of April 24, 2026; AUM Rs 57,033 cr (largest Nifty 50 ETF in India); expense ratio 0.04% (industry-leading low). Tracks Nifty 50 - which closed at 23,995.70 on April 28, 2026 (-0.4%, -97 pts). Sensex closed at 76,886.91 (-417 pts, -0.54%). 5-year CAGR 12.68% as of April 22, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Broad-market sentiment under multiple pressures: (1) RBI ECL framework finalized April 28 tightens NPA/provisioning starting April 2027 - hit Nifty Bank by 864 pts that day; (2) US-Iran geopolitical tensions and rising Brent crude; (3) persistent FII outflows; (4) 26% US reciprocal tariff overhang on Indian goods. Mid/small-caps showing relative resilience over benchmarks. Earnings season is a counterweight (HINDZINC, M&MFIN, MARUTI strong; IIFL weak).
stocks.peer_comparison: NIFTYBEES is the largest, most-liquid Nifty 50 ETF by AUM (Rs 57,033 cr) with the lowest expense ratio (0.04%). Tracks the same underlying as competing ETFs (ICICI, HDFC, SBI Nifty 50 ETFs) but with a structural cost edge. 97.88% large-cap, 2.12% mid-cap; top sector exposure: Banks 29.13%.
red_flags: Banking-heavy composition (29.13% top sector) directly exposed to RBI ECL framework provisioning hit
FII outflow regime continues to pressure large-cap index
26% US reciprocal tariff macro overhang on Indian equities
Nifty has broken below psychological 24,000 support
catalysts: Strong Q4 earnings prints from Nifty constituents (HINDZINC, M&MFIN, MARUTI, NESTLEIND)
Domestic flow support (SIP/DII) historically buffers FII selling
Potential resolution of US-Iran tensions could ease crude/FII pressure
RBI rate path - any dovish surprise lifts large-cap multiples

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced April 24, 2026: Consolidated PAT Rs 5,033 cr (+68% YoY); Revenue Rs 13,544 cr (+49% YoY); EBITDA margin 57% (best-ever). FY26 PAT Rs 13,832 cr (+33.6%), FY26 Revenue Rs 40,844 cr (+19.8%). FY27 first interim dividend of Rs 11/share (550% on Rs 2 face value, +10% vs Rs 10 last year), total payout Rs 4,648 cr. RECORD DATE TOMORROW (April 30, 2026 - ex-date). Highest-ever quarterly mined metal production 315 kt; refined metal 282 kt; zinc production cost at 16-quarter low of $994/ton. Stock at Rs 616.10 on April 28 (down from Rs 628 on April 27).
stocks.sector_context: LME zinc surged to $3,430-3,472/MT in April 2026 - highest in years. LME zinc inventory fell to 113,950 mt on April 2 with cancelled warrants up 301.87% - tight supply. Goldman/Citi/Macquarie bullish $3,200-3,600/ton for 2026, potential $4,000 if stocks fall further (Morgan Stanley more cautious at $2,900). ILZSG forecasts global refined zinc demand +1% to 13.86 mn MT in 2026. Mine shutdowns and smelter cuts driving structural tightness.
stocks.peer_comparison: Industry-leading 57% EBITDA margin places HINDZINC at top of metals/mining peer group; outperforming Vedanta and other base-metal producers on margin and operational efficiency. Silver segment expected to contribute up to 50% of EBITDA soon - unique optionality vs pure-play zinc peers. CEO Arun Misra signaled HZ business demerger planning may begin in FY27.
red_flags: Stock weakened from Rs 628 on April 27 to Rs 616.10 on April 28 - some unwinding into ex-dividend
Vedanta parent demerger record date May 1, 2026 - HZ NOT part of demerger but parent restructuring noise persists
Commodity price cyclicality - Morgan Stanley sees $2,900/ton avg, below current spot
Stock had rallied ~21% in month before results - entry timing matters
catalysts: Interim dividend Rs 11/share record date TOMORROW (April 30, 2026) - last day of cum-dividend trading
Sustained zinc price strength ($3,400+/MT) supports FY27 earnings momentum
Silver segment scaling to ~50% of EBITDA - structural margin tailwind
Production cost at 16-quarter low ($994/ton) - operating leverage
FY27 demerger optionality flagged by management

stocks.recent_news: Stock at ~Rs 295 in April 2026. Q4 FY26 results scheduled May 19, 2026 (board meeting); consensus expects Revenue Rs 12,000-12,800 cr (vs Rs 11,866 cr in Q3 FY26) and PAT Rs 3,800-4,100 cr. Board meeting TOMORROW (April 30, 2026) to consider unsecured rupee term loan / line-of-credit fundraise. Q3 FY26 PBT Rs 5,238.79 cr (+6.7% YoY); transmission revenue Rs 11,801.23 cr (+5.93% YoY). New CMD Burra Vamsi Rama Mohan effective April 1, 2026 (33 yrs transmission/telecom experience), with additional charge as Director (Projects) for 3 months. Two independent directors (Shiv Tapasya Paswan, Rohit Vaswani) completed tenure April 15, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Indian power sector among April 2026's strongest. India met record peak demand of 256.1 GW on April 25, 2026 with no shortage; electricity consumption April 1-27 grew 8.9% YoY. National Electricity Plan 2023-32 targets 458 GW peak by 2032 - massive transmission capex pipeline. Government RAISED POWERGRID's permissible equity investment per subsidiary from Rs 5,000 cr to Rs 7,500 cr - enables larger UHV transmission projects. NTPC Green crossed 10 GW capacity end-March, validating renewable transmission demand.
stocks.peer_comparison: Power sector seeing up-to-24% upside in select names (NTPC, Adani Power, Tata Power). POWERGRID's regulated-equity model offers higher earnings visibility than IPP peers. Trades at ~Rs 295 vs analyst consensus 12-month target of Rs 315-400 (high Rs 388-460, low Rs 220-260). Buy rating from 13 of 24 analysts, 8 Hold, 3 Sell.
red_flags: Two independent directors completed tenure April 15 - governance bench-strength bears watching pending replacements
CMD holding additional charge as Director (Projects) creates concentration risk during transition
Q3 FY26 earnings growth of +6.7% YoY is steady but unspectacular - not a high-growth narrative
Broader equity market under 26% US reciprocal tariff overhang and FII outflows
catalysts: Board meeting TOMORROW (April 30, 2026) for unsecured fundraise - capacity for large project pipeline
Q4 FY26 results May 19, 2026 with FY26 final dividend recommendation
Government raised equity investment ceiling to Rs 7,500 cr/subsidiary - enables UHV / Kenya PPP scale-up
Record 256.1 GW peak demand validates transmission capex thesis
Hot summer driving Q1 FY27 demand outlook
